question: Will many open source advocates agree frontier models have crossed capability thresholds too dangerous to open source?
reason: Recent debates and expert opinions indicate a growing concern among open source advocates about the risks of releasing frontier AI models.
evidence: Lawrence Lessig, a prominent open-source advocate, expressed concerns about the risks associated with open-weight AI models, likening them to a 'bomb' that could be misused. ([transformernews.ai](https://www.transformernews.ai/p/lawrence-lessig-open-source-ai-risks?utm_source=openai))

The OECD/GPAI report highlights that AI openness exists on a spectrum, with open-weight models revealing only a fraction of the information required for full accountability, suggesting that many open source advocates may agree on the dangers of releasing frontier models. ([oecd.ai](https://oecd.ai/en/wonk/balancing-innovation-transparency-and-risk-in-open-weight-models?utm_source=openai))

A report by the National Telecommunications and Information Administration (NTIA) endorses open-source AI but acknowledges the risks of misuse, indicating that open source advocates are aware of the potential dangers. ([ccn.com](https://www.ccn.com/news/technology/open-source-ai-deepfake-dangers/?utm_source=openai))
